steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The opening lines state the narrator's core claim: "I understood things about the baby that her mother could not accept." The rest of the passage supports this — the narrator sees that Dot is "impatient with babyhood," while Celestine "never saw that" and only valued Dot's "helpless softness."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To present the narrator's belief that she understands Dot better than Celestine does.: This matches exactly. The narrator claims a "mental connection" with Dot and explicitly contrasts her own understanding with what Celestine "could not accept" or "never saw." ✓
  • Choice B — To speculate that when Dot is older, her personality will be like Celestine's.: The passage draws a contrast between Dot and Celestine, not a similarity. No prediction about Dot's future personality is made. ✗
  • Choice C — To discuss what the narrator and Celestine do to amuse Dot.: The passage is about how they perceive Dot, not what they do to amuse her. The narrator and Celestine are not described doing activities together. ✗
  • Choice D — To compare the narrator's physical appearance to the physical appearance of Celestine.: No physical descriptions of the narrator or Celestine appear in the text. ✗
A is correct.
